report_metadata.subject: Storage Layer Design for MCP Code Knowledge Server
report_metadata.current_storage: flat JSON files under .knowledge/ (index.json, symbols.json, dependencies.json, summaries/cache.json)

current_data_shape.KnowledgeIndex: single JSON object with module list, lastBuilt, fileCount, symbolCounts
current_data_shape.SymbolEntry: array of ~72 objects (26-file codebase); each has qualifiedName, file, line, signature, type, calls[], calledBy[], isExported
current_data_shape.DependencyGraph: { nodes[], edges[{from,to,type}], cycles[][], fileDeps: Record<file, file[]> }

rationale: The codebase is a single-process Node.js MCP server communicating over stdio — no network layer, no concurrency from multiple writers.
SQLite's embedded model means zero external infrastructure, matching the existing zero-dependency deployment model.
better-sqlite3 uses synchronous APIs, which eliminates async overhead on every tool call and keeps handler code simple (current handlers are already sync in find-callers.ts, get-dependencies.ts).
SQLite supports indexed JOINs, recursive CTEs for graph traversal (WITH RECURSIVE), and FTS5 for full-text search on summaries — capabilities the current flat-JSON approach lacks entirely.
For 26–500 files the entire database fits comfortably in the OS page cache; query latency is sub-millisecond for indexed lookups.
Migration is non-breaking: the existing JSON files can be generated as a compatibility export alongside the SQLite DB, or the tool handlers can be updated to hit SQLite transparently.
Rebuild frequency (on code change) is low; SQLite handles atomic batch writes via transactions far better than rewriting multi-MB JSON files.

in_memory_json.reason: Current approach: entire symbols.json is read and filtered on every find_symbol call (O(n) scan). At 72 symbols/26 files it is fast enough, but at 500 files with 1000+ symbols the linear scan degrades and the 30–200 KB files incur repeated parse overhead. No persistent index, no FTS, no graph queries.

redis.reason: Requires a running Redis server. Adds infrastructure complexity incompatible with the single-binary MCP deployment. No benefit over SQLite for a single-process reader.

leveldb.verdict: Acceptable but inferior to SQLite for this use case
leveldb.reason: LevelDB is a key-value store; graph traversal and relational joins require application-level secondary indexes. No SQL, no FTS5, more code complexity. Better suited for append-heavy time-series workloads.

postgres.reason: Network-connected RDBMS for a local MCP process serving 26–500 files. Adds server management, connection pool overhead, and deployment complexity with no benefit.

schema_design.engine: SQLite 3 with WAL mode, FTS5 extension

files.description: One row per indexed source file. Stores computed summary fields and LLM-generated descriptions.
files.ddl:
CREATE TABLE files (
  id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  path            TEXT    NOT NULL UNIQUE,   -- relative path, e.g. 'src/lib/foo.ts'
  module          TEXT    NOT NULL,           -- parent module name, e.g. 'src'
  purpose         TEXT,                       -- static one-line purpose
  detailed_purpose TEXT,                      -- richer static description
  llm_description TEXT,                       -- LLM-generated (expensive, cached)
  architectural_role TEXT,
  complexity_score REAL,
  content_hash    TEXT    NOT NULL,           -- SHA-256 of file content at index time
  last_modified   INTEGER NOT NULL,           -- Unix ms timestamp
  last_indexed    INTEGER NOT NULL,           -- Unix ms timestamp
  exports         TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',  -- JSON array of export names
  dependencies    TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',  -- JSON array of raw import strings
  side_effects    TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
  throws          TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
  internal_patterns TEXT  NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
  test_files      TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
  richness_level  TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'minimal'  -- 'minimal'|'standard'|'rich'
);
notes: path is the lookup key matching current cache keys in summaries/cache.json
Storing JSON arrays as TEXT columns (exports, dependencies) avoids a separate junction table for small arrays; parse cost is negligible for <50 entries
content_hash enables incremental rebuild: skip re-indexing if hash unchanged

symbols.description: One row per extracted symbol. Replaces symbols.json array.
symbols.ddl:
CREATE TABLE symbols (
  id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  qualified_name  TEXT    NOT NULL UNIQUE,  -- 'OrderService.createOrder'
  name            TEXT    NOT NULL,          -- 'createOrder'
  file_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES files(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  file_path       TEXT    NOT NULL,          -- denormalized for fast lookup without JOIN
  line            INTEGER NOT NULL,
  signature       TEXT    NOT NULL,
  type            TEXT    NOT NULL,          -- 'function'|'class'|'interface'|'type'|'method'|'module-init'|'decorator'|'enum'|'constructor'
  module          TEXT    NOT NULL,
  is_exported     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- 0|1 (SQLite has no boolean)
  language        TEXT,
  jsdoc           TEXT,
  return_type     TEXT,
  parameters      TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',  -- JSON: ParameterDoc[]
  decorators      TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
  throws          TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
  complexity      REAL,
  calls           TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',    -- JSON: qualifiedName[]
  called_by       TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'     -- JSON: qualifiedName[] (inverted index)
);
notes: calls and called_by are stored as JSON arrays to mirror existing SymbolEntry shape; the call_edges table (below) provides the normalized graph for traversal queries
file_path is denormalized to avoid a JOIN on every find_symbol result formatting step

call_edges.description: Normalized call graph. Enables recursive CTE traversal without deserializing JSON arrays.
call_edges.ddl:
CREATE TABLE call_edges (
  id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  caller_id   INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES symbols(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  callee_id   INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES symbols(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  caller_qname TEXT   NOT NULL,  -- denormalized for fast text lookup
  callee_qname TEXT   NOT NULL,
  UNIQUE(caller_id, callee_id)
);
notes: Replaces the O(n) BFS in find-callers.ts that currently builds a Map<string, SymbolEntry> on every call
WITH RECURSIVE CTE over this table gives transitive closure in a single SQL query

dep_edges.description: File-level import dependency graph. Replaces DependencyGraph.edges and DependencyGraph.fileDeps.
dep_edges.ddl:
CREATE TABLE dep_edges (
  id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  from_file   TEXT    NOT NULL REFERENCES files(path) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  to_file     TEXT    NOT NULL,  -- may reference external packages (not in files table)
  edge_type   TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'direct',  -- 'direct'|'dynamic'
  UNIQUE(from_file, to_file)
);
notes: Enables WITH RECURSIVE for transitive dependency queries replacing bfsTraverse() in get-dependencies.ts
to_file may not exist in files table (external npm packages); no foreign key enforced on to_file

modules.description: One row per logical module (directory group). Replaces KnowledgeIndex.modules list.
modules.ddl:
CREATE TABLE modules (
  id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  name         TEXT    NOT NULL UNIQUE,  -- 'src', 'scripts', 'mcp-server'
  entry_points TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',  -- JSON: file paths
  exports      TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',  -- JSON: exported symbol qualified names
  description  TEXT
);
notes: Lightweight table; module membership is derived from files.module column at query time

knowledge_meta.ddl:
CREATE TABLE knowledge_meta (
  id               INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK (id = 1),
  last_built       INTEGER NOT NULL,   -- Unix ms
  file_count       INTEGER NOT NULL,
  has_symbols      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  has_dependencies INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  build_generation INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  build_in_progress INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  richness_level   TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'minimal',
  schema_version   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1
);

summary_fts.description: FTS5 virtual table for full-text search across file summaries and LLM descriptions.
summary_fts.ddl:
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE summary_fts USING fts5(
  path,
  purpose,
  detailed_purpose,
  llm_description,
  architectural_role,
  jsdoc_text,      -- concatenated jsdoc from all symbols in the file
  content='files',
  content_rowid='id',
  tokenize='porter unicode61'
);
notes: Porter stemmer handles plural/verb forms (e.g. 'authenticate' matches 'authentication')
Kept as a shadow of the files table; trigger-maintained or rebuilt on each index run
Replaces current search-architecture.ts which does line-by-line regex over architecture.md

primary_indexes.complexity: O(log n + k) where k = files in module

primary_indexes.replaces: symbolMap.get(qualifiedName) — currently built as a Map<string, SymbolEntry> on every find-callers invocation

primary_indexes.type: CREATE INDEX idx_symbols_name ON symbols(name COLLATE NOCASE)
primary_indexes.query: SELECT * FROM symbols WHERE name LIKE ? COLLATE NOCASE
primary_indexes.complexity: O(log n) for exact, O(log n + k) for prefix; LIKE '%foo%' still O(n) — see full-text note
primary_indexes.notes: Case-insensitive B-tree index accelerates exact and prefix lookups; substring match (current behavior) still requires FTS or scan

primary_indexes.type: CREATE INDEX idx_symbols_name_type ON symbols(name COLLATE NOCASE, type)
primary_indexes.query: SELECT * FROM symbols WHERE name LIKE ? AND type = ?
SELECT * FROM symbols WHERE file_id = (SELECT id FROM files WHERE path = ?)
primary_indexes.replaces: symbols.filter(s => s.file.endsWith('/' + key)) — O(n) in get-implementation-context.ts

primary_indexes.query: SELECT * FROM symbols WHERE module = ? AND is_exported = 1

primary_indexes.type: CREATE INDEX idx_call_edges_callee ON call_edges(callee_id); CREATE INDEX idx_call_edges_caller ON call_edges(caller_id)
primary_indexes.query: WITH RECURSIVE callers(id, depth) AS (SELECT caller_id, 1 FROM call_edges WHERE callee_id = ? UNION ALL SELECT ce.caller_id, c.depth+1 FROM call_edges ce JOIN callers c ON ce.callee_id = c.id WHERE c.depth < ?) SELECT s.qualified_name, s.file_path, s.line, c.depth FROM callers c JOIN symbols s ON s.id = c.id
primary_indexes.complexity: O(E log V) for recursive CTE with index; E = edges, V = symbols
primary_indexes.replaces: bfsCallers() in find-callers.ts — JS loop loading entire symbols.json

primary_indexes.type: CREATE INDEX idx_dep_from ON dep_edges(from_file); CREATE INDEX idx_dep_to ON dep_edges(to_file)
primary_indexes.query: WITH RECURSIVE deps(file, depth) AS (SELECT to_file, 1 FROM dep_edges WHERE from_file = ? UNION ALL SELECT de.to_file, d.depth+1 FROM dep_edges de JOIN deps d ON de.from_file = d.file WHERE d.depth < ?) SELECT * FROM deps

symbol_name_collision_strategy.example: If two files both export 'handler', they become 'find-symbol.handler' and 'find-callers.handler' — distinct qualified names
symbol_name_collision_strategy.substring_search: find_symbol uses name LIKE '%query%' which returns all matches across files; results ranked by name length (shorter = more exact) as in current implementation
symbol_name_collision_strategy.index_note: LIKE '%foo%' with leading wildcard cannot use B-tree index; for substring match, use FTS5: SELECT path FROM symbol_fts WHERE symbol_fts MATCH 'foo*' OR add a trigram index (SQLite 3.38+ supports trigram FTS5 tokenizer)

graph_traversal.method: WITH RECURSIVE CTE on call_edges / dep_edges tables
graph_traversal.cycle_safety: Add WHERE c.depth < maxDepth bound (already the design); alternatively detect cycles via NOT IN visited subquery
graph_traversal.performance_note: For 500-file codebases with ~2000 symbols and ~5000 edges, recursive CTE completes in <5ms with indexes

full_text_search.method: FTS5 virtual table summary_fts with porter tokenizer
full_text_search.query_example: SELECT path, rank FROM summary_fts WHERE summary_fts MATCH 'authentication service' ORDER BY rank LIMIT 20
notes: FTS5 rank() function provides BM25 relevance scoring out of the box
Index is rebuilt atomically at the end of each knowledge build run
jsdoc_text column concatenates all symbol JSDoc strings for the file to enable symbol-description search without a separate symbols FTS table

migration_plan.strategy: Additive parallel store — JSON files remain the source of truth during migration; SQLite is introduced as an accelerated read layer

phases.name: Introduce SQLite alongside JSON
phases.description: Add a write step at the end of the build-knowledge script that: (a) opens/creates .knowledge/knowledge.db, (b) runs the schema DDL idempotently, (c) bulk-INSERTs all data from the freshly-written JSON files inside a single transaction.
phases.tool_changes: None — all MCP tool handlers continue reading JSON files.

phases.name: Migrate data-loader.ts to SQLite reads
phases.description: Replace loadSymbols(), loadDependencies(), loadSummaryCache(), loadIndex() in tools/lib/data-loader.ts with SQLite equivalents using better-sqlite3. The function signatures remain identical — callers are untouched.
phases.tool_changes: Internal to data-loader.ts only. All 18 tool handlers call these four functions; zero changes needed in tool files.
phases.interface_compatibility: loadSymbols() returns SymbolEntry[] — the SELECT query reconstructs the same shape. loadSummaryCache() returns Record<string, FileSummary> — a simple SELECT * FROM files converts to the same record shape.
phases.risk: Low. All existing tool handler tests/assertions remain valid.

phases.name: Replace JS BFS with SQL recursive CTEs
phases.description: Update find-callers.ts and get-dependencies.ts to run WITH RECURSIVE queries instead of building in-memory Maps and BFS queues.
phases.tool_changes: Internal logic only. Input/output contracts (FindCallersArgs -> CallToolResult) remain unchanged.
phases.risk: Low-medium. Requires integration testing of recursive CTE correctness against existing BFS output.

phases.name: Replace search-architecture.ts with FTS5
phases.description: Replace regex line search over architecture.md with a SELECT query against summary_fts. The architecture.md content is ingested into the FTS table at build time.

phases.name: Deprecate JSON artifacts (optional)
phases.description: Once Phase 2-4 are stable, remove JSON file writes from the build script. Keep content_hash and last_modified in files table for incremental rebuild logic that previously relied on summaries/cache.json content hashes.
phases.risk: Medium — any external tooling that reads .knowledge/*.json directly breaks. Recommend keeping JSON as a compatibility export.

migration_plan.backward_compatibility_note: Phases 1-2 are fully backward compatible. The existing KNOWLEDGE_ROOT env var convention is preserved — knowledge.db lives at path.join(KNOWLEDGE_ROOT, 'knowledge.db').

cache_design.overview: Three distinct TTL tiers reflect the cost and stability of each data category

llm_summaries.invalidation_trigger: files.content_hash change detected during incremental rebuild
llm_summaries.rationale: LLM calls (Anthropic/Ollama) are expensive (API cost + latency). A file rarely changes meaning without changing content. Hash-based invalidation ensures correctness; wall-clock TTL is a safety backstop for stale hashes.
llm_summaries.storage_note: content_hash and last_indexed are stored in the files table; incremental-updater.ts should compare hashes before triggering LLM re-summary

symbol_records.ttl_human: No TTL — always rebuilt on any source file change
symbol_records.invalidation_trigger: Any file in files table has content_hash mismatch with current disk content
symbol_records.rationale: Symbol extraction is purely static analysis (no API cost). Recomputing is fast (<1s for 500 files with the TypeScript adapter). Stale symbols cause incorrect call graphs which break find-callers reliability — freshness is critical.
symbol_records.rebuild_strategy: Incremental: only re-extract symbols for files whose content_hash changed. DELETE FROM symbols WHERE file_id = ? then re-insert.

dependency_edges.ttl_human: No TTL — rebuilt on any import/export change
dependency_edges.invalidation_trigger: content_hash change in any file that has dep_edges rows
dependency_edges.rationale: Import graph extraction is cheap (regex/AST parse of import statements). Stale edges cause wrong transitive dependency answers. Medium cost because a single file change can affect many downstream edges.
dependency_edges.rebuild_strategy: Incremental: DELETE FROM dep_edges WHERE from_file = ? for changed files, then re-insert.

file_index_metadata.fields: knowledge_meta table, files.purpose, files.exports, files.dependencies (static fields)
file_index_metadata.ttl_human: No TTL — rebuilt on any file change
file_index_metadata.rationale: Static analysis fields (exports list, dependency list, throws) are cheap to recompute. The knowledge_meta.last_built timestamp is always updated at end of build run.

in_process_read_cache.description: Optional: a Node.js Map<string, {data, ts}> in the MCP server process holding the last loaded SymbolEntry[] and DependencyGraph
in_process_read_cache.rationale: With better-sqlite3's synchronous API, SQLite reads are already fast (<1ms). A 30s in-process cache only makes sense if health_check shows the same symbols.json being loaded 5+ times per minute during an active agent session. The TTL is short enough that a knowledge rebuild (triggered by code change) becomes visible within one tool-call cycle.
in_process_read_cache.invalidation: Check knowledge_meta.build_generation integer; if changed, invalidate in-process cache immediately

query_patterns.name: Symbol lookup by name (substring)
query_patterns.current_implementation: JSON.parse(symbols.json) then Array.filter() — O(n) every call
query_patterns.sql: SELECT s.*, f.path FROM symbols s JOIN files f ON s.file_id = f.id WHERE s.name LIKE ? COLLATE NOCASE ORDER BY length(s.name) ASC LIMIT 20
query_patterns.expected_performance: <2ms with idx_symbols_name; O(log n + k) for prefix, O(n) for mid-string wildcard
query_patterns.optimization_note: For mid-string substring search, add a trigram FTS5 tokenizer or accept O(n) on 500-row table (still <1ms)

query_patterns.name: Symbol lookup by name + type filter
query_patterns.current_implementation: Same O(n) filter with added type check
query_patterns.sql: SELECT * FROM symbols WHERE name LIKE ? COLLATE NOCASE AND type = ? ORDER BY length(name) LIMIT 20

query_patterns.name: File summary by path (exact)
query_patterns.current_implementation: Object key lookup on cache Record — O(1) hash; but requires full JSON parse of cache.json first (O(file_count))

query_patterns.name: File summary by path (suffix match)
query_patterns.current_implementation: Object.keys(cache).find(key => key.endsWith('/' + input)) — O(n) scan in path-utils.ts:findSummary()
query_patterns.sql: SELECT * FROM files WHERE path = ? OR path LIKE ('%/' || ?)
query_patterns.expected_performance: <2ms; LIKE with leading wildcard is O(n) but n<=500 is fast; can add trigram index if needed

query_patterns.name: All symbols in a file
query_patterns.current_implementation: symbols.filter(s => s.file.endsWith('/' + key)) — O(n) scan every call
query_patterns.sql: SELECT * FROM symbols WHERE file_id = (SELECT id FROM files WHERE path = ?) AND type != 'module-init' LIMIT 15

query_patterns.name: Direct callers of a symbol (depth=1)
query_patterns.current_implementation: Build Map<qualifiedName, SymbolEntry> (O(n)), then iterate calledBy[] array
query_patterns.sql: SELECT s.qualified_name, s.file_path, s.line FROM call_edges ce JOIN symbols s ON s.id = ce.caller_id WHERE ce.callee_id = (SELECT id FROM symbols WHERE qualified_name = ? COLLATE NOCASE)

query_patterns.name: Transitive callers of a symbol (depth>1)
query_patterns.current_implementation: BFS loop over calledBy JSON arrays — O(V + E) but requires full symbols.json parse
query_patterns.sql: WITH RECURSIVE callers(sym_id, depth) AS (SELECT caller_id, 1 FROM call_edges WHERE callee_id = (SELECT id FROM symbols WHERE qualified_name = ? COLLATE NOCASE) UNION ALL SELECT ce.caller_id, c.depth + 1 FROM call_edges ce JOIN callers c ON ce.callee_id = c.sym_id WHERE c.depth < ?) SELECT s.qualified_name, s.file_path, s.line, c.depth FROM callers c JOIN symbols s ON s.id = c.sym_id ORDER BY c.depth
query_patterns.expected_performance: <5ms for depth<=5 on a 2000-symbol graph with idx_call_edges_callee

query_patterns.name: Transitive dependencies of a module/file
query_patterns.current_implementation: Build adj Map + BFS loop over JSON edges array — O(V + E) with full dependencies.json parse
query_patterns.sql: WITH RECURSIVE deps(file, depth) AS (SELECT to_file, 1 FROM dep_edges WHERE from_file = ? UNION ALL SELECT de.to_file, d.depth + 1 FROM dep_edges de JOIN deps d ON de.from_file = d.file WHERE d.depth < ?) SELECT file, MIN(depth) as depth FROM deps GROUP BY file ORDER BY depth
query_patterns.expected_performance: <5ms for depth<=3 on 500-file graph with idx_dep_from

query_patterns.name: Full-text search on file summaries
query_patterns.current_implementation: Regex line scan over architecture.md — limited to a single static file
query_patterns.sql: SELECT path, snippet(summary_fts, 1, '', '', '...', 15) as excerpt, rank FROM summary_fts WHERE summary_fts MATCH ? ORDER BY rank LIMIT 10
query_patterns.expected_performance: <5ms for BM25 FTS5 query over 500 documents with porter tokenizer
query_patterns.bonus: Enables semantic search across all file summaries and LLM descriptions, not just architecture.md

implementation_notes.open_flags: OPEN_READWRITE | OPEN_CREATE with WAL journal mode for concurrent reads during build
implementation_notes.build_transaction: All INSERTs during a knowledge rebuild should be wrapped in a single BEGIN IMMEDIATE ... COMMIT transaction. At 500 files this completes in <100ms vs. 500 individual transactions.
implementation_notes.schema_migration: Check knowledge_meta.schema_version on open; if < current version, run ALTER TABLE / CREATE TABLE migrations in a transaction. Bump schema_version at the end.
implementation_notes.file_to_add: scripts/lib/db.ts — centralized SQLite open/init/migration module used by both build scripts and MCP tools
implementation_notes.approximate_db_size: For 500 files with ~2000 symbols, ~5000 dep edges, ~3000 call edges: estimated 2-8 MB SQLite file, well within single-file embedded database sweet spot
